hero.question: Will Israel strike 3 countries in 2026?
hero.prob_take: At 6.4%, the crowd has basically written this off as noise, which means the market is pricing in either sustained regional de-escalation or the belief that Israel's military calculus stops at two targets max. The real question is whether the definition of "strike" (air raids, covert ops, or boots on ground) tightens the aperture, and whether markets are anchoring too hard on 2025's trajectory rather than the full year ahead.

trade.question: When will DHS be funded again?: Will legislation that, upon becoming law, results in the Department of Homeland Security being funded at 12:01 AM ET on the later of (i) February 14, 2026 and (ii) the calendar day after enactment become law before Apr 8, 2026?

daily_take.headline: Three-Country Strike Odds Crater. Market Pricing Out Regional War Scenario.
daily_take.deck: Israel striking 3 countries in 2026 just dropped 6.5 points to 6.4%. The move signals cooling tension after weeks of Middle East escalation, but the floor feels too low. A sharp bettor asks: does a 6.4% probability reflect genuine de-escalation or just recency bias from a quiet news cycle? Iran tensions stay live. This is value if you think 2026 still has legs for a broader conflict.

sidebar.headline: US-Iran direct engagement odds barely moved on the three-country drop, suggesting markets see different escalation paths. This disconnect is worth exploiting.
sidebar.label: US Forces Enter Iran: 14.0%

sidebar.headline: Astros ALCS odds cratered 7.5 points in 24 hours on what's likely just regular preseason noise. Check if injury reports warrant the repricing or if this is panic selling.
sidebar.label: Houston Astros ALCS Win: 6.5%

sidebar.headline: Anduril IPO odds fell 22 points hard. The defense tech darling may have pulled guidance or signaled later timing. Worth investigating if the move is news-driven or pure sentiment reversal.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO Before 2027: 25.5%
